title: RRB Group D 2026
meta_description: RRB Group D 2026 — Track Maintainer, Helper, Porter, and other Level 1 posts in Indian Railways. 10th pass eligibility, salary ₹18-21K, physical test, and how to prepare.

hero.one_liner: Level 1 posts in Indian Railways — Track Maintainer, Helper, Porter, and Gateman for 10th pass candidates with ITI preferred

what_is_it: RRB Group D is one of the largest recruitment drives in India — typically offering 1,00,000+ vacancies for Level 1 posts in Indian Railways. Posts include Track Maintainer Grade-IV, Helper in various railway workshops, Porter, Pointsman, Gateman, and other operational roles that keep India's vast railway network running.

The exam requires 10th pass qualification (ITI/equivalent is preferred for some trades and gives an edge). The selection process includes: Computer Based Test (CBT), Physical Efficiency Test (PET), and Document Verification. The CBT is at a basic level — 10th class standard — testing Math, General Intelligence, General Science, and General Awareness.

Despite the large number of vacancies, competition is intense — typically 1-2 crore candidates apply. However, the exam difficulty is low, and consistent preparation for 3-4 months is sufficient for most candidates to clear the CBT cutoff. The physical test is the tougher hurdle for many applicants.

Railway Group D jobs come with all railway benefits: free rail travel passes, medical facilities, quarters/HRA, pension (NPS), and job security. Starting salary at Level 1 is ₹18,000-21,000/month including allowances. Promotions to Group C posts are possible through departmental exams.

key_details.label: Education
key_details.value: 10th pass from recognized board. ITI in relevant trade preferred (gives advantage in selection).

key_details.label: Physical Test (Male)
key_details.value: 35 kg weight lift + carry 100m in 2 min. Run 1000m in 4 min 15 sec.

key_details.label: Physical Test (Female)
key_details.value: 20 kg weight lift + carry 100m in 2 min. Run 1000m in 5 min 40 sec.

key_details.label: Medical
key_details.value: Distant vision 6/9 (without glasses), adequate color vision, good hearing.

key_details.label: Selection
key_details.value: CBT → Physical Efficiency Test (PET) → Document Verification → Medical

exam_pattern.description: 100 MCQs across 4 sections at 10th class difficulty level. No sectional time limit. Bilingual (English + Hindi).

extra_sections.heading: Physical Efficiency Test — Detailed Requirements
content: The PET is the stage where maximum candidates get eliminated. Prepare physically from day one of your preparation:
Male candidates: (1) Must lift and carry 35 kg weight for 100 meters in 2 minutes without putting it down. (2) Must run 1000 meters (1 km) in 4 minutes 15 seconds on flat ground.
Female candidates: (1) Must lift and carry 20 kg weight for 100 meters in 2 minutes without putting it down. (2) Must run 1000 meters (1 km) in 5 minutes 40 seconds.
PwBD candidates: Exempted from PET based on the nature of disability.

Training tips: Start running daily — begin with 2 km and build up to 3 km in under 12 minutes. Practice weight carrying with a sandbag or heavy bag at home. The weight carry is more about technique than strength — practice walking fast (not running) with the weight on your shoulder/back. Build endurance with daily practice for at least 2-3 months before PET date.

important_dates.label: Notification
important_dates.value: Check rrbcdg.gov.in for latest Group D notification

important_dates.label: Application
important_dates.value: Online only through respective RRB zone website

preparation_tips: Focus on General Science — it carries 25% weightage and questions come from NCERT Class 6-10 Science. Revise basic physics (motion, force, energy, light, electricity), chemistry (acids-bases, metals, carbon compounds), and biology (human body, diseases, nutrition, ecology).

Mathematics is at 10th class level. Master: number system, BODMAS, percentage, ratio-proportion, profit-loss, simple-compound interest, time-work, time-distance, and basic geometry. 30 minutes of daily math practice is sufficient.
Physical preparation is as important as written exam preparation. Start running and weight-carrying practice 3 months before the PET date. Many candidates clear the written exam but fail PET.
Previous year papers (2018-19 and 2022 cycles) are freely available — solve at least 20 papers. The pattern and difficulty remain consistent across cycles.

faqs.question: Group D salary seems low. Is it worth it?
faqs.answer: At Level 1, salary starts at ₹18,000 + allowances = ₹18,000-21,000/month. But add: free rail travel (worth ₹50,000+/year), free medical (worth ₹30,000+/year), quarters or HRA, pension. Total value of compensation is ₹30,000+/month equivalent. For 10th pass in tier-2/3 India, this is a solid government career.

faqs.question: Can I appear for Group D and NTPC both?
faqs.answer: Yes! If you're 12th pass, you can appear for both Group D (10th qualification) and NTPC (12th/graduation posts). Many candidates prepare for both since the syllabus overlaps significantly. NTPC offers higher posts if you clear it.

faqs.question: Track Maintainer — what does the job involve?
faqs.answer: Track Maintainers work on maintaining railway tracks — inspecting tracks for defects, replacing damaged rails/sleepers, maintaining track alignment, and ensuring track safety. It's physically demanding outdoor work, often in extreme weather. Shifts include night duties. Despite being demanding, it's one of the most vacancy-heavy posts in Indian Railways.

faqs.question: Is ITI mandatory for Group D?
faqs.answer: No, 10th pass is sufficient. However, ITI in relevant trades (Fitter, Welder, Electrician, etc.) gives preference in selection for workshop-related posts. If you have ITI, mention it in your application — it improves your chances for better post allocation.
